title: practical english usage - Part 122
content:
That can show that something has come  to an end.

(     ... this-Seottish boy you-used-te go out with.) Have you ever heard from that Scottish boy you used to go out with?

Did you see that? Who said that?

That was nice. What was it? (     This was-niee...) are more distant in the past.

That/those can refer to situations and events which  have just finished, or which Watch this. This is a police message.

Listen to this. You'll like it. (     Listen to that...) I like this music. What is it?

start.

This/these can refer to situations and events which  are going on or just about to 4 time I like those earrings. Where did you get them?

All the time I was in that country I hated it.

That smells nice - is it for lunch? Get that cat off the piano.

speaker, or not present.

We   use that/those for people and things which  are more distant from the Do you like these earrings? Joseph gave them to me.

I don't know what I'm doing in this country, (     . .. in that country.) Get this cat off my shoulder.

This is very nice - can I have some more?

We   use this!these for people and things which  are close to the speaker.

For a similar use of it to refer to people, ►     173.9.

Note also Those who ... (►   144.7 below).

Who's that? These are the Smiths.

Hello. This is Elisabeth. Is that Ruth? That looks like Mrs Walker.

people are.

However, this, etc can be used without nouns for people when we  say who  the the;   my,    your,          this,    that,           Section Determiners:       a/an   and           etc; etc          12 grammar •  145  this/that and it. things that have just been mentioned (More natural than ...  That was a turning point...) the two men entered into a partnership which lasted until 1946, and...

Then in 1917 he met Andrew Lewis. This was a turning point in his career: they took her to court, believe it or not. The case came up last week ...

So she decided to paint her house pink. This upset the neighbours so much  that This is preferred when there is more to say about the new subject of discussion.

as you can imagine.

So she decided to paint her house pink. This/That really upset the neighbours, or situations, suggesting ’ an interesting new fact has been mentioned’ .

This and that are more emphatic; they 'shine a light', so to speak, on the things So she decided to paint her house pink. It upset the neighbours a bit.

just been talked or written about. It does not give any special emphasis.

This, that and it can all be used to refer back to things or situations that have 1  referring       back been    mentioned 145  this/that        and   it:  things     that    have    just For these and those with singular kind of, sort of, ►    592.       For that which, ►    237.21 .

come and go (►   424) and bring and take (►  409).       For this one, that one, etc, ►     182.

The  differences between this and that are similar to the differences between here and there (►  484), Earn more money during those long winter evenings. Call...

When you get that empty feeling - break for a snack.

This use is common in advertisements.

I can't stand that perfume of hers.

That/those can suggest that an experience is familiar to everybody.

There was this travelling salesman, you see. And he wanted...

story-telling.

Note the special use of this (with no demonstrative meaning) in conversational 9 other     uses 'How was the play?' 'Not all that good.' Not all that can be used to mean 'not very'.

It was so cold that I couldn't feel my fingers, (     It-was-that cold that...) In standard English, only so is used before a following clause.

If your boyfriend's that clever, why isn't he rich?

1 didn 't realise it was going to be this hot.

the same way as so.

In an informal style, this and that are often used with adjectives and adverbs in 8  this     and   that     meaning       'so' Those who can, do. Those who can't, teach.

A dog's intelligence is much  greater than that of a cat.

mean 'the one(s)'. Those who . .. means 'the people who  .. / In a formal style, that and those can be used with a following description to 7 that,     those      meaning       'the   one(s)' grammar •  145  this!that and it: things that have just been mentioned For more about it, ►     173.
